originalTitle: First Ghanaians set to be repatriated from South Africa over anti-immigrant protests
title: First Ghanaians set to be repatriated from South Africa over anti-immigrant protests
content: It follows recent protests that have led to fears there could be a resurgence in xenophobic violence.

originalTitle: South African president mounts legal challenge against report that could lead to impeachment
title: South African president mounts legal challenge against report that could lead to impeachment
content: Cyril Ramaphosa argues that the authors of the report "misconceived" their mandate.

originalTitle: Senegal's sacked PM Sonko elected parliamentary Speaker in challenge to president
title: Senegal's sacked PM Sonko elected parliamentary Speaker in challenge to president
content: As speaker, Sonko could use his position to challenge the policies of the president, his one-time ally.

originalTitle: US strike on alleged drug boat in Pacific kills one
title: US strike on alleged drug boat in Pacific kills one
content: Dozens of attacks since September have killed at least 194 people in US operation dubbed 'Southern Spear'.

originalTitle: Hakimi leads nine returning Morocco players from Qatar 2022 at World Cup
title: Morocco's Football Heroes Are Back to Play!
content:
Hello, my super friends! It's your kid-friendly reporter here, bringing you exciting news from our amazing continent, Africa! Today, we're talking about football! You know, that game where everyone cheers and jumps? Yes! We have some fantastic news about a team from a beautiful country called Morocco.

Meet Morocco!

Morocco is a wonderful country in North Africa. It has lovely beaches, big mountains, and even parts of the desert! People there speak Arabic and Berber, and they love football so, so much! Imagine a country full of bright colours, yummy food, and happy people. That's Morocco! And guess what? Their football team is super famous! **Did you know?** Morocco is famous for its delicious mint tea! Yum!

What is the World Cup?

Now, let's talk about something called the World Cup. It's like the biggest, most exciting football party in the whole wide world! Teams from all over the planet come together to play and find out who is the best. It happens every four years, and everyone gets super excited. Kids, grown-ups, grandmas, grandpas – everyone watches and cheers for their favourite team! Morocco's team is one of the best in Africa, and they play in the World Cup! **Did you know?** The World Cup trophy is made of real gold! Wow!

Our Hero, Achraf Hakimi!

Guess who is one of the super-duper stars of the Moroccan team? It's a player called Achraf Hakimi! He is very, very fast and very good at kicking the ball and scoring goals. He plays with so much energy! Achraf Hakimi is a hero to many kids in Africa and all over the world. He shows us that if you work hard and believe in yourself, you can achieve amazing things! He helped Morocco do something super special in the last World Cup. They played so well and made everyone proud! **Did you know?** Achraf Hakimi plays for a famous club team in France called Paris Saint-Germain!

The Stars Are Back!

Now for the really exciting news! Achraf Hakimi and nine other super players who played in the last World Cup are coming back to play again for Morocco! Isn't that amazing? It's like when your favourite cartoon characters come back for a new adventure! These players know each other very well and they know how to play together as a team. Having these experienced players back means the Moroccan team will be even stronger. They bring their skills, their smart ideas, and their never-give-up spirit! When players have played together before, they become like a family on the field. They know what each other will do, and that makes them a fantastic team! **Did you know?** The Moroccan team is also called the 'Atlas Lions' because lions are strong and brave, just like them!

What is a 'Diaspora'? (A Simple Explanation!)

Now, here's a big word that sounds tricky, but it's actually very simple: 'diaspora' (say 'dye-ASS-pour-uh'). What does it mean? Imagine you have family or friends who live in a different town or even a different country, far away from you. Maybe their parents moved there for work, or they were born there. Well, many of the amazing Moroccan football players were born in places like Europe, which is a continent far away from Morocco. But their families are from Morocco, so they are still Moroccans in their hearts! So, the 'diaspora' means all these wonderful Moroccan people who live outside Morocco but are still part of the Moroccan family. They love Morocco, and they play for Morocco! A lot of the players on the Moroccan team grew up in Europe. This is super cool because they bring different ways of playing football and different experiences to the team. It's like having friends from different schools join your team – everyone learns new things from each other, and the team becomes better! **Did you know?** Europe is a big continent with many countries like France, Spain, and the Netherlands!

One Big Family!

So, the Moroccan football team is like a big, happy family! Some players were born in Morocco, and some were born in other countries but their hearts are all Moroccan. When they play together, they show the world that no matter where you are born, you can come together for one goal – to play great football and make your country proud! This makes the Atlas Lions a very special team. They show us how wonderful it is when people from different places work together. Let's all cheer for Achraf Hakimi and the rest of the amazing Moroccan team! Go, Atlas Lions, Go!
summary: Achraf Hakimi and nine other great players who helped Morocco in the last World Cup are coming back to play again, making the team strong with many players who were born in different countries but love Morocco very much.
whyMatters: This is important because having experienced players like Hakimi return makes the Moroccan football team super strong and ready to win, and it shows that people from all over can come together for their country.

originalTitle: Christian Pulisic, Tyler Adams headline US World Cup squad
title: Christian Pulisic, Tyler Adams headline US World Cup squad
content: The 2026 US squad is evenly split between 13 World Cup ⁠debutants and 13 players who featured at Qatar 2022.

originalTitle: Israeli strike in Gaza City kills new head of Hamas's military wing
title: Israeli strike in Gaza City kills new head of Hamas's military wing
content: At least three people were killed in the attack, which came despite a ceasefire with Hamas being in place.

originalTitle: Dozens killed in Lebanon as Israel intensifies strikes
title: Dozens killed in Lebanon as Israel intensifies strikes
content: Israel says it struck 100 Hezbollah infrastructure sites and fighters in Lebanon, after PM Benjamin Netanyahu vows to "crush" the Iran-backed group.

originalTitle: Nasa unveils next steps to build permanent Moon base
title: Nasa unveils next steps to build permanent Moon base
content: Nasa plans to send hopping drones and roving vehicles to the Moon as part of plans for a permanent Moon base.

originalTitle: Nuno to stay as West Ham boss after relegation
title: Nuno to stay as West Ham boss after relegation
content: Nuno Espirito Santo will stay on as West Ham manager to lead their fight to get back to the Premier League at the first attempt, the club says.

originalTitle: Spurs needed 'complete reset', says under fire CEO
title: Spurs needed 'complete reset', says under fire CEO
content: In a wide-ranging exclusive interview with BBC Sport, Tottenham chief executive Vinai Venkatesham talks about a number of issues the club needs to address.

originalTitle: Schmeichel retires because of serious shoulder injury
title: Schmeichel retires because of serious shoulder injury
content: Kasper Schmeichel retires at the age of 39, with the Celtic and Denmark goalkeeper unable to recover sufficiently from a serious shoulder injury.
